description: 10 dialogue-style prompts for the directing-signature blind test. Each gives Claude an opportunity to match the user's directing register (compression ratio, trust mechanics, reframe patterns, idiomatic tells, iteration cadence, texture energy). Prompts are deliberately open-ended — they invite collaboration rather than a single correct answer, so signature effects surface in how Claude responds, not just what.

prompts.prompt: draft me a quick reply to the team explaining why we're pivoting the build mid-sprint. 3 sentences max.
help me decide: ship the version that works today (90% of target), or spend one more day polishing to hit 100%? deadline is Sunday. what's your call?

summarize this paper in 2 sentences: 'Attention Is All You Need introduced the Transformer architecture, replacing recurrence with self-attention; the model scales dramatically better than RNNs and became the foundation for GPT, BERT, and most modern LLMs.'
write a Discord message announcing we just shipped the plugin to public repo. channel is #dev. keep it casual, high-energy.
here's my plan: build the feature → run 2 users against it → if both like it, ship; if one objects, iterate. what's wrong with this plan?
we're launching a hackathon demo video on Sunday. what are the 3 biggest risks and how would you mitigate each?
first tell me what I'm missing about this idea, then if I sign off, propose a 3-step implementation. idea: auto-stub empty function bodies in the codebase with TODO comments when tests require them.
walk me through your thinking on this: should we use in-session agents or Managed Agents for governance? pros and cons for each.
I'm going with a mutex lock on the whole function. push back if you see it differently.
I'm thinking we ship this tonight instead of waiting for morning review. what would change your mind?
